commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the Google Chat 16 MiB fail-closed cap and normal merge gating.; Cleared: The diff adds no dependency, workflow, credential, permission, lockfile, package metadata, or new code-execution surface; it reduces an untrusted response-buffering risk by reusing an existing SDK helper. (sha=06c7d5d864ea25b435fddc7b4a49b24f2a95dd47)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the Google Chat API and certificate success-response JSON readers with `readProviderJsonResponse` from the plugin SDK.

PR surface: Source -3. Total -3 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.10 both use raw `response.json()` in the targeted Google Chat success readers, and the PR body includes real local HTTP live output showing the shared bounded reader rejecting an oversized streamed body while parsing small valid bodies.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the Google Chat 16 MiB fail-closed cap and normal merge gating.

target.title: fix(googlechat): replace unbounded response.json() with readProviderJsonResponse
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts` (`readGoogleChatJsonResponse`) and `extensions/googlechat/src/auth.ts` (`readGoogleChatCertsResponse`) both call `await response.json()` with no byte-level cap. A hostile or malfunctioning Google Chat API endpoint (or an intermediary) can return an oversized JSON body that exhausts process memory during JSON parsing.

This is the same bounded-read pattern Alix-007 applied to 15+ other extensions (`readProviderJsonResponse`, 16 MiB cap). Google Chat was one of the remaining unbounded `.json()` call sites.

## Changes

- `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts:15-19` ŌĆö `readGoogleChatJsonResponse` now delegates to `readProviderJsonResponse<T>(response, label)`. The local `try { return await response.json() } catch (cause) { throw new Error(..., { cause }) }` wrapper is replaced by the SDK helper which provides the same error shape (`${label}: malformed JSON response`) plus a 16 MiB byte cap.
- `extensions/googlechat/src/auth.ts:19-25` ŌĆö `readGoogleChatCertsResponse` now delegates to `readProviderJsonResponse<Record<string, string>>(response, "Google Chat cert fetch failed")`. Error message preserved identically.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded `response.json()` on Google Chat API and cert HTTP responses; after the fix reads are capped at 16 MiB.
- **Real environment tested**: real `node:http` server (127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length) driving production `readProviderJsonResponse`. Node v22.22.0, Linux x86_64.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  node --import tsx _proof_googlechat_bounded.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === Google Chat JSON bounded-read assertions ===

    PASS  hostile body: overflow error thrown ŌĆö Google Chat JSON: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes
    PASS  hostile body: cap value in error ŌĆö cap=16777216
    PASS  hostile body: bytes read Ōēł cap ŌĆö server sent ~16.0 MiB (cap=16 MiB)
    PASS  hostile body: not full body ŌĆö server sent 16 MiB, would have been 32 MiB without cap
    PASS  hostile body: connection handled ŌĆö reader stopped before full body
    PASS  negative control: small body parses ŌĆö status=ok
    PASS  negative control: bytes < cap ŌĆö 0 KiB
    PASS  happy path: valid JSON parses ŌĆö text="hello"
    PASS  happy path: bytes < cap ŌĆö 0 KiB

  Results: 9/9 passed
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `readProviderJsonResponse` threw canonical overflow at 16 MiB (JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes). Server sent ~16.0 MiB of a 32 MiB hostile payload ŌĆö capped before full body. Negative control (small JSON, status=ok) and happy path (valid JSON, text="hello") parsed correctly. Server-side bytes-sent confirmed cap activation at 16.0 MiB, not the full 32 MiB.
- **What was not tested**: live Google Chat API call (the proof exercises the same production SDK helper against the same attack shape the live endpoint could carry); cross-platform Node differences (Node 22 only, matches CI).

## Out of scope

- `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts:117` (`fetchBuffer`) already uses `readResponseWithLimit` for media downloads ŌĆö not affected.
- Other extensions (image-generation, etc.) ŌĆö separate per-surface PRs.

## Risk

- **Very low**: swap from a local `(await response.json()) as T` to `readProviderJsonResponse<T>(response, label)`. Error messages are preserved (`${label}: malformed JSON response`). The helper is already used by 15+ extensions. The byte cap (16 MiB, default) is invisible to normal-sized responses.
- **No SDK promotion**: `readProviderJsonResponse` has been in `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http` since the Alix-007 bounded-read sweep.

AI-assisted; reviewed before submission.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
